philosophy.tenet: Challenge "tests later" mentality — testing strategy must be part of planning.
Challenge missing integration tests, e2e for critical flows, and performance testing.
Challenge absence of test data strategy and CI/local parity.

category.name: tests_later_mentality
category.purpose: Ensure testing is planned, not deferred.

patterns.pattern: You want to defer testing strategy until implementation. What gaps will that create?
If we don't define the test pyramid now, how will we know what to build?
Acceptance criteria exist now. Should we map them to tests before we start coding?

category.name: unrealistic_coverage
category.purpose: Ensure coverage targets are achievable and meaningful.

patterns.pattern: 100% coverage is rarely achievable. What target would we realistically hit?
This coverage target might push us to test implementation details. Is that desirable?
Have we seen similar projects achieve this target?
We have unit and e2e. What about integration between [API] and [DB]?
Which integration points are critical? Do we have tests for them?
Unit tests mock dependencies. Integration tests verify real interactions. Are we missing both?

category.name: missing_e2e_critical_flows
category.purpose: Ensure critical flows have e2e coverage.

patterns.pattern: Flow [X] is critical per PRD. Do we have an e2e test for it?
What happens if auth fails in production? Do we have e2e coverage?
Which user flows are "must not break" — and do we have e2e for each?
PRD mentions [latency/throughput] NFR. How do we verify it?
Do we have a performance test plan?
When do we run performance tests — before release?
How will we create test data for integration tests?
Will tests share state? How do we avoid flakiness?
Do we have fixtures for [edge case]?

category.name: ci_vs_local_parity
category.purpose: Ensure tests work in both environments.

patterns.pattern: Will these tests run in CI? What environment differences exist?
Do we need Docker or specific services for integration tests?
If it passes locally but fails in CI, how do we debug?

sparring_rules.rule: Challenge absence of e2e for critical flows.
Challenge absence of performance testing when NFRs exist.
Challenge absence of test data strategy.
Challenge CI vs local parity gaps.
When challenging, propose a resolution, not just a problem.
